nodes.name: Google Sheets - Read Leads
nodes.notes: SETUP REQUIRED: Select the same 'Digital Artifacts Leads' Google Sheet from Workflow A

parameters.subject: The one question
parameters.message:
={{ $json.first_name }},

Isaiah here from Digital Artifacts. Thanks for reaching out.

Quick way to figure out if an AI operating system is worth your time: what's one task your team does every week that's currently eating the most hours? If it's repeatable (same inputs, same outputs, just done weekly), it's a candidate for the 4-layer stack we build.

Reply with the task and I'll tell you honestly whether it's worth automating or whether it's a human-judgement job.

Or book 15 minutes if you'd rather talk: https://calendar.app.google/PLACEHOLDER

Isaiah
hello@digitalartifacts.com.au

Unsubscribe: {{ $json.unsub_link }}

parameters.subject: Why AI that works is different
parameters.message:
={{ $json.first_name }},

Quick follow up. The single reason most AI deployments stall at small businesses: the AI has no memory of who you are.

Every conversation starts from scratch. It forgets clients, positioning, pricing logic. It hallucinates because it is guessing, not reading.

What we build is different. Layer 1 (Context) is your business written down as markdown files. Positioning, clients, pricing, tone, SOPs. The AI reads it at runtime but never writes to it. Correct a fact once, every future output is right. We call this rule "humans write, AI reads" and it is what separates a neat demo from something you trust.

Worth a conversation?
https://calendar.app.google/PLACEHOLDER

Isaiah

Unsubscribe: {{ $json.unsub_link }}

parameters.subject: The four layers, concretely
parameters.message:
={{ $json.first_name }},

One more note on how this actually works day to day.

Take a professional services firm. Their 4-layer stack:
Layer 1 (Context): client histories, matter files, proposal templates, billing rates.
Layer 2 (Skills): reusable prompt recipes. proposal-writer, meeting-prep, client-update.
Layer 3 (Automation): 8:30am Slack brief with today's matters. RFP inbound triggers a proposal scaffold. Meeting transcript auto-drafts into structured notes.
Layer 4 (Agent): Slack for everything. Dashboard for partners.

Same framework, different deployment for a trades operator, or a sales team, or a logistics business.

If this pattern fits something on your plate, 15 minutes is enough to scope it: https://calendar.app.google/PLACEHOLDER

Isaiah

Unsubscribe: {{ $json.unsub_link }}

parameters.subject: Still thinking about it?
parameters.message:
={{ $json.first_name }},

No rush at all, just checking in. Still considering scoping a 4-layer build, or is the timing off right now?

If it is off, reply anything and I will drop you from follow-ups. If you want a 15-minute call to sketch what this could look like for your business, here: https://calendar.app.google/PLACEHOLDER

Isaiah

Unsubscribe: {{ $json.unsub_link }}

parameters.subject: A free 4-layer sketch
parameters.message:
={{ $json.first_name }},

One offer I do not make publicly: book a 30-minute call and I will map your business's Layer 1 brain (what to put in it), three Layer 2 skills (which tasks to automate first), two Layer 3 workflows (what triggers them), and one Layer 4 surface (where your team talks to it). Free, no obligation.

Even if you do not build it with me, you walk away with a clear architecture you can hand to anyone.

https://calendar.app.google/PLACEHOLDER

Isaiah

Unsubscribe: {{ $json.unsub_link }}

parameters.subject: Off the list?
parameters.message:
={{ $json.first_name }},

Last email in this sequence. If AI operating systems are relevant someday but not now, no problem. I will take you off the list so your inbox stays clean.

If you want to stay in my orbit (I send a short monthly note with new patterns and worked examples), do nothing and I will keep you in. Otherwise click below and you are off:

{{ $json.unsub_link }}

Either way, good luck with whatever you are building.

Isaiah
